code: @Manual{,   title = {kcData: Access Kansas City census data},   author = {Eliot Monaco},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.0.0.9008},   url = {https://eliotmonaco.github.io/kcData/}, }

title: Access Kansas City census data
text: kcData simplifies downloading using US Census Bureau data Kansas City.

title: Access Kansas City census data
text: Install GitHub:

title: Geographic identifiers (GEOIDs) — geoid
text: Geographic identifiers (GEOIDs) Kansas City intersecting regions. census tracts ZCTAs, GEOIDs included 10% geometry's area within Kansas City boundary. See US Census Bureau page Understanding Geographic Identifiers information GEOIDs.

title: Geographic identifiers (GEOIDs) — geoid
text: list character vectors containing GEOIDs state 2-digit state FIPS code cbsa 5-digit core-based statistical area code place 2-digit state FIPS code 5-digit place FIPS code county 2-digit state FIPS code 3-digit county FIPS code tract2010 2-digit state FIPS code, 3-digit county FIPS code, 6-digit census tract code, 2010-2019 tract2020 2-digit state FIPS code, 3-digit county FIPS code, 6-digit census tract code, 2020-2024 zcta2010 5-digit ZIP code tabulation area code, 2010-2019 zcta2020 5-digit ZIP code tabulation area code, 2020-2024

title: Geographic identifiers (GEOIDs) — geoid
text: US Census Bureau TIGER/Line Shapefiles

what: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
text: Download population data Kansas City decennial census American Community Survey (ACS). Summary levels place, county, census tract, census block group, census block (decennial census ), ZIP code tabulation area (ZCTA). Data sets downloaded US Census Bureau using tidycensus package.

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
code: get_kc_pop(   dataset,   geo = c("place", "county", "tract", "block group", "block", "zcta"),   year,   vars,   var_match = c("fixed", "regex"),   geoids = NULL,   ... )

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
text: dataset data set download. Passed dataset argument tidycensus::load_variables() either survey tidycensus::get_acs() sumfile tidycensus::get_decennial(). geo data set geography (summary level). year data set year. vars variables download. Either vector variable names regular expression pattern. var_match Use "fixed" match variable names vars exactly "regex" match regular expression pattern. geoids vector GEOIDs filter output. ... Additional arguments passed tidycensus::get_acs() tidycensus::get_decennial(), key user's census API key.

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
text: dataframe.

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
text: ACS census variables can explored Census Reporter website using tidycensus::load_variables() download variables specific data set. GEOIDs can obtained US Census Bureau shapefiles data set geoid package. default, state = 29 included call relevant tidycensus function.

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
text: Decennial census website American Community Survey website List US Census Bureau data sets available via API Census Reporter (ACS tables variables reference)

title: Get population data for Kansas City — get_kc_pop
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Download place-level data from the 2024 1-year ACS acs1_2024_place <- get_kc_pop(   dataset = "acs1",   geo = "place",   year = 2024,   vars = "^B01",   var_match = "regex",   geoids = geoid$place,   key = keyring::key_get("census-api-key") )  # Download ZCTA-level data from the 2023 5-year ACS acs5_2023_zcta <- get_kc_pop(   dataset = "acs5",   geo = "zcta",   year = 2023,   vars = "B01001_001",   var_match = "fixed",   geoids = geoid$zcta2020,   key = keyring::key_get("census-api-key") )  # Download census block-level data from the 2020 census block <- get_kc_sf(   geo = "block",   year = 2024,   intersect = "city",   geometry = "full" )  geoid_block <- block$GEOID20  census_2020_block <- get_kc_pop(   dataset = "dhc",   geo = "block",   year = 2020,   vars = "P12_001N",   var_match = "fixed",   geoids = geoid_block,   county = sub("^29", "", geoid$county),   key = keyring::key_get("census-api-key") ) } # }

what: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
text: Download TIGER/Line shapefile containing Kansas City city boundary Kansas City metro region boundary set smaller geographies intersecting one : county, census tract, census block group, census block, ZIP code tabulation area (ZCTA) Shapefiles downloaded US Census Bureau using tigris package.

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
code: get_kc_sf(   geo = c("cbsa", "place", "county", "tract", "block group", "block", "zcta"),   year,   intersect = c("city", "metro"),   geometry = c("clipped", "full") )

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
text: geo shapefile geography. "place" returns city boundary "cbsa" returns metro region boundary. year shapefile year. intersect geo "place" "cbsa", larger geography determines intersection geography selected geo. "metro" selected, geographies Kansas Missouri  returned. geometry geo "place" "cbsa", return either full geometries geometries clipped geography indicated intersect.

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
text: dataframe class sf containing simple feature list column.

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
text: Geographies smaller city metro region can contain full geometry geometries clipped city metro region boundary. geographies, geometry = "clipped" two additional variables added feature's full area area within city metro region boundary.

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
text: TIGER/Line Shapefiles TIGER/Line Files Technical Documentation Standard Hierarchy Census Geographic Entities tigris package

title: Get simple features objects for Kansas City — get_kc_sf
code: if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{ # Get the 2024 city boundary city <- get_kc_sf(geo = "place", year = 2024)  # Get 2024 census tracts clipped to the city boundary tracts <- get_kc_sf(geo = "tract", year = 2024)  # Get full 2024 ZCTAs that intersect with the KC metro region zctas <- get_kc_sf(   geo = "zcta",   year = 2024,   intersect = "metro",   geometry = "full" ) } # }

title: Intercensal population estimates, 2010-2020 — popest_ic20
text: Kansas City population estimates 2010-2019. According US Census Bureau, "produced, intercensal estimates become preferred series data decade."

title: Intercensal population estimates, 2010-2020 — popest_ic20
text: geographic_area Area name est_base_apr_1_2010 April 1, 2010, resident population estimates base est_[year] Resident population estimate July 1 year census_apr_1_2020 April 1, 2020, census population count

title: Intercensal population estimates, 2010-2020 — popest_ic20
text: US Census Bureau, City Town Intercensal Population Totals: 2010-2020

title: Vintage 2024 population estimates — popest_v24
text: Kansas City population estimates 2021-2024. According US Census Bureau, "Vintage 2024 recent completed vintage consistent set estimates."

title: Vintage 2024 population estimates — popest_v24
text: name Area name est_base_2020 April 1, 2010, resident population estimates base est_[year] Resident population estimate July 1 year

title: Vintage 2024 population estimates — popest_v24
text: US Census Bureau, City Town Intercensal Population Totals: 2020-2024

title: Get population estimates — return_popest_table
text: Create table population estimates using intercensal population estimates (popest_ic) recent population estimate vintages (popest_v<YY>).

title: Get population estimates — return_popest_table
text: years numeric vector years.

title: Get population estimates — return_popest_table
text: dataframe.
